decisions_made.decision: Three broad implementation phases (T1 → T2 → T3) instead of micro-step decomposition
decisions_made.rationale: Fixture explicitly requests minimal code changes that are easy to verify; broad phases align with this requirement and simplify review.

decisions_made.decision: No external knowledge queries or exploration required
decisions_made.rationale: Synthetic fixture is self-contained, deterministic, and explicitly designed for workflow testing without external dependencies. All AC are explicit and actionable.

decisions_made.decision: 1:1 mapping of tasks to acceptance criteria (T1→AC1, T2→AC2, T3→AC3)
decisions_made.rationale: Each AC describes a distinct phase of work with clear dependencies; straightforward mapping ensures traceability and avoids over-engineering.

context_confidence_notes: High confidence based on: deterministic synthetic fixture, explicit ACs without external references, self-contained scenario, no ADO dependencies, clear acceptance criteria, and prior successful intake stage execution.
summary: Task plan generated for synthetic workflow smoke test. Three sequential tasks created to implement local synthetic fixture support in the agent-runner workflow. All acceptance criteria covered. No external dependencies or exploration required. High context confidence (95%).
